messages.content:
CVE-2022-4468 is a medium severity vulnerability found in the WP Recipe Maker WordPress plugin versions prior to 8.6.1. The vulnerability stems from improper validation and escaping of shortcode attributes before they are output back onto the page. This flaw allows users with lower-level roles, such as contributors, to execute Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.

The potential impact is significant as it permits attackers to target higher-privileged users, including admins, which could lead to unauthorized actions or access within the system. This vulnerability is associated with the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1203, which relates to exploitation for client execution.

The affected software is specifically the WP Recipe Maker WordPress plugin versions before 8.6.1. To mitigate this vulnerability, it is recommended to update to the latest version of the plugin. Implementing controls M1050 (Enterprise Policy), M1051 (User Training), and M1048 (Application Isolation and Sandboxing) are also advisable to reduce potential exploitation risks.
